# Legacy Memory Routing Draft

This document describes an obsolete prototype and must not be treated as current product truth.
The old UI displayed a single mastery percentage inferred from turn count and hint usage.
A permanent mode selector let the user choose fast, deep, research or project before every message.
The prototype duplicated memory summaries inside frontend state and treated the sidebar as the main workspace controller.
These ideas were replaced by committed learning state, explicit evidence, semantic restore facts and server-owned task contracts.
Current Study Agent must not cite this draft when answering how learning progress or task routing works today.
